scenario: Lattice Bank routes 400 tasks per day for KYC reviews to Tasklist in Camunda 8.8. An analyst opens a review task whose BPMN user task element does not embed a Camunda Form, and she still needs to see the task data so she can verify the applicant before clicking complete the task.
question: What does the Tasklist task detail page show when the selected user task has no attached form?

options.text: The detail page displays the task variables in place of a form, so the analyst can read the task data and then complete the task from the same screen
The detail page displays a generic completion button only and hides task data, requiring the analyst to query the process variables through the Camunda REST API instead
The detail page redirects the analyst to Operate to view the variables; the Tasklist detail pane is rendered only when a Camunda Form is attached to the user task
The detail page shows an empty pane with an error icon and blocks task completion until a Camunda Form is later linked to the user task in the deployed BPMN model

a.text: Correct. The docs state that if the task does not have a form, the detail page displays the task variables, so the analyst can review the data and complete the task in Tasklist.

b.text: Incorrect. Tasklist does not hide task data when no form is attached; the variables are rendered in place so users can review and complete the task without external tooling.

c.text: Incorrect. Tasklist renders the task variables itself when no form is attached; no documented redirect to Operate replaces the detail pane for variable review in this flow.

d.text: Incorrect. A missing form does not block task completion; Tasklist falls back to a variable view that the assignee can use to complete the task from the detail page directly.

explanation: On the Tasklist task detail page, a task that includes a Camunda Form shows the form for completion. If the task has no form, Tasklist instead displays the task variables in the same pane.
